title: syntax-rules: nested ellipsis (x ... ...) unsupported AND fails non-fatally -> silent WRONG value with exit 0
goal: Found by the P6e metaprogramming depth harness (2026-07-03). Two problems. (1) Nested/double ellipsis in a template is unsupported: ((_ (row ...) ...) (+ 0 row ... ...)) and ((_ (row ...) ...) (list (list row ...) ...)) both report 'misplaced ellipsis in macro template' and 'Undefined variable: row'. This is a real R7RS feature (a pattern variable at ellipsis depth 2 used under two ellipses). (2) CRITICALLY, the error is NON-FATAL on the -r/AOT path: the compiler prints the errors to stderr but still exits 0 and runs a mis-expanded body -- e.g. (mat (1 1) (1 1)) yields RESULT: 0 (just the (+ 0) seed) instead of 4. A macro-expansion error that leaves a silently-wrong program with exit 0 hides the failure. nested_ellip family: max-correct-depth 0.
acceptance: either: nested ellipsis (row ... ...) expands correctly so (define-syntax flt (syntax-rules () ((_ (r ...) ...) (+ r ... ...)))) (flt (1 2) (3 4)) => 10
or (minimum): a macro-expansion error is FATAL -- nonzero exit, no wrong RESULT emitted -- on both -r and AOT
no case where 'misplaced ellipsis' is printed yet the program exits 0 with a wrong value
campaign_payoff: Silent wrong answers with exit 0 are the worst failure mode; nested ellipsis is standard R7RS macro power.
repro: printf "(define-syntax flt (syntax-rules () ((_ (r ...) ...) (+ r ... ...))))\n(display (flt (1 2) (3 4)))(newline)\n" > /tmp/t.esk; build/eshkol-run -r /tmp/t.esk; echo exit=$?  # errors on stderr but exit 0, wrong/zero value
resolution: Fixed at the architectural root in lib/frontend/macro_expander.cpp (branch fix/nested-ellipsis). Pattern matching now records each pattern variable's full ellipsis-nesting shape in a MatchTree (depth 0 = scalar; depth N = list of depth N-1 subtrees per repetition) instead of flattening into a single value list. A shared matchPatternSeq engine handles top-level macro-call args and nested MACRO_PAT_LIST sub-patterns identically, and binds zero-repetition ellipsis variables to empty sequences (collectPatternVarDepths). On the template side, consecutive '...' markers are counted; expandEllipsisElementN iterates the driver binding's repetitions via peelBindingsAtIndex and flattens (splices) one extra level per extra ellipsis, so (+ r ... ...) and (list (list row ...) ...) both expand correctly at depth 2 and 3 (arbitrary depth by construction). (flt (1 2) (3 4)) => 10 on both -r and AOT; nested_ellip P6e family max-correct-depth 0 -> 48 both modes; no 'misplaced ellipsis' printed for valid nested-ellipsis templates. Regression: macros suite 7/7, SICP smoke 88/88, qq_macro_depth_test (PR #144) still OK. Tests: tests/metaprog/nested_ellipsis_test.esk (12 checks, -r + AOT).
